candidates.why: The visible depreciation/amortization rows sit under SG&A-related sections (Selling expenses and Business development and administrative expenses), not under Cost of goods sold. No explicit COGS-specific D&A row is shown before EBIT.
Generic row with no visible operating bucket assignment; not clearly tied to COGS and should not be added to avoid double counting.
Generic row with no visible operating bucket assignment; not clearly tied to COGS and should not be added to avoid double counting.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortisation row is identified as belonging to Cost of goods sold / COGS. Conservative conclusion: COGS D&A is already embedded in the selected COGS row or not separately disclosed in the excerpt, so nothing should be added separately.

candidates.why: This is an SG&A D&A summary label, but the selected SG&A parent rows appear to already include their D&A components. For example, Selling expenses equals its balancing value plus depreciation/amortization, and Business development and administrative expenses also equals balancing value plus depreciation/amortization.

Appears as a component beneath selected SG&A parent rows (Selling expenses and Business development and administrative expenses). Because those selected parent rows already include these amounts, adding them separately would double count.
This appears to be a component of Research and development costs, which is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD. Under the R&D interaction rule, it should not be added separately.

variables.notes: The SG&A-related depreciation/amortisation rows appear to be sub-components embedded within the already selected parent rows: Selling expenses, Business development and administrative expenses, and Research and development costs. Therefore no separate XSGA_DA row should be added.
